review_text: Dig it was awesome for the kids! Well priced and 2 hours of fun!

Just tried to book this place as we are coming up at the end of the year and this is the first place that has refused to take a deposit then pay the rest off at a later date.

Originally I was told that there is nothing they could do as it's policy for full payment to be taken upfront.

I have since been contacted by one of the hotels senior managers who was really understanding and was able to help

They have advised they are working on updating the system to offer a deposit service which will be great

It's just a shame I had to write a negative review to get someone's attention

We look forward to staying here 😌
Awesome
Food was good and reasonably priced,  zip line was fun

50min drive Logan, good place for kids with multiple activities.
Thrilling activities for Kids which require adult. Food stealing Kookaburras in Cafe, Creek Water Pools below , close to Mt Tamborine mountain top for sunrise/sunset observation.
Plenty of Parking available and Onsite Hotel rooms.
Had a great time there with the family. Only downside is that some of the cabins need a bit of an update as they look a bit worn out in some places.

The owners have done a wonderful job, it's perfect for adults and children.  Every detail thought through - ensuite at the camping sites are exceptionally designed, totally pet friendly (even at restaurant and spa!) really good coffee at the cafe + GF options, activities for the children brilliant esp junior zip lining well explained and supervised to be challenging AND safe for the kids.  This is a wonderland.  Well done to the staff and owners, brilliant experience.

The free bird feeding at 2 pm was a really nice surprise. It was fun to see the birds up close，a simple activity, but it made the visit much more enjoyable. Definitely worth checking out if you’re there in the afternoon.
Fun place to be very friendly to all groups and family. Their pickle ball court rental and mini golf are very affordable.
Lot of fun for kids and grownups

My kids had an awesome time on the Junior TreeTop Challenge. We didn't see our eldest the entire time, and the staff were amazing with our youngest who needed a little help and confidence boost at times! Absolutely fantastic team
Great place to take the family for a day or weekend, heaps of stuff to do, there's low ropes course and a high ropes course and thunder eggs everywhere.

Thunderbird park- Cool place.. small tools for mining, but good for kids.. I got blisters so id recommended taking gloves and also definitely a hat and water, it does get hot especially when digging.  They have a cutting machine to cut your stones you find and a small shop with stones to look at or buy..
For like $10? you can get a bag of sand, filled with coloured crystals to pan and find yourself theyre pretty rich in stone chips- great to give the kids a cool experience, and guaranteed to get something.

They also have real working mini excavators for the kids or adults? To experience moving dirt and from what I could see there was a wrecking ball type thing too to either knock stuff off or practice placement carefully? I didn't actually stick around to watch.. but looked fun for kids..
We Didn't do or see any other stuff but im sure there's more..
I would recommend going for a cool day out with kids and a different experience..

A big shout out to Shai and Callum for the zipline experience, great experience and a great adventure. Highly recommended. I also took the time to review the reviews prior to attending and I was most impressed with the management/owner and genuine responses to feedback. Most impressive, keep up the great work guys.
Wonderful breakfast buffet, even if your not staying there, rock up and enjoy

Was a little dissapointed... Granted I did zero research, and was spur of the moment visit but the entrance really gave off a dinosaur vibe and had hyped up the lil one that we were going to see dinosaurs.. to which we were sorely let down. He did have fun running around but not what I had expected.

And also very weary of the kids playground at the cafe... I doubt it meets safety regulations, with the gate being so bent from the shade clothes hanging off the support pole that it can't close and latch even if you tried.

The crystal panning was fun for the kids. But there was a charge for everything. I asked to bring a cake and could only do so if booking the restaurant. Cutting fee is $5 per person and cancellation fee was $25 per person. We opted to cake the cake in the cafe area but we're told we cannot borrow a knife and they don't have lighters for candles.

Went for a fossick at the thunder egg mine. Had a fantastic time digging for pretties. My only suggestion is bring gloves so you don't get blisters 🤣 spent three solid hours digging. Definitely worth a trip 😃👌💜 friendly staff.
Good day out with the grandkids. Found a good thunder egg. Busy time.

Great place for families. We were camping there for Australian Day long weekend with big group of friends. We had powered sizes and good amoof share which was so important during extremaly hot days. The natural rock poolside and Cristal Clear and cold water was amazing and refreshing.  A lot of additional activities included some without extra charge. Great facilities. I think we will be back one day again! :)

The staff are INCREDIBLE! The team are absolutely beautiful, and make you feel so welcome. The gentleman who works at the front desk at the Canyon Flyer, is so bubbly and friendly, and as soon as we arrived he made us feel so at ease. Huge shoutout to our guides, Melody and Sam, who were so so great!! They made our group feel super safe whilst ziplining. I even conquered my fear of heights! Our group also did some fossicking, and the tall, curly haired gentleman who works at the Rock Shop is so good at his job and incredibly knowledgeable. My cousin ended up getting scraped up when we went up the hill and he couldn’t have been more helpful. Definitely will be back!
It’s good if you are going to do top tree walking.
It is really nice all the staff and it's near the IGA and got a nice creek
This is inside the Treetop challenge
Great place to visit so close to nature!

Visited for just the day. This was our 3rd visit and have noticed a drop in service. Very disorganised at the mine site check in.  Now getting more expensive to go. Big plus was the Cafe service, quick and efficient even with the busy day. Food selection was normal fare but well prepared and tasty. You have to eat in the Cafe as they discourage you from bringing food as there is no picnic tables or BBQ areas.

Good exhausting day out.  Did the Tree Top Challenge and that's what it was.  Had a good time.  Gear was in good condition.  Hair nets provided. Guides were friendly and helpful. Toilets...need some work.  Not for the couch potato;-)

We took our 3 year old Grandson to Dig It. We had a lovely time on the mountain. With the little one in tow, 1 activity was enough. Cost was very reasonable for the time. We had a nice but expensive snack. Decent shoes  is a must as paths are dirt.

We had a great time up at Thunderbird Park. As a family, we did the Geode Fossicking. It was fairly priced for what it was, a bit of fun, but my kids found it very hard work. They were given bouts of motivation when they found something cool though.
Take your swimmers as the creek was really nice to cool off in on a hot day.
We had lunch there aswell, which was on the higher priced side, but as a tourist destination its to be expected.
The service we received from any of the staff there though was awesome. Everyone was friendly and helpful.
Can't wait to go back for other activities or even to camp.
Awesome place to visit. Plenty to do there and you can finish the day with a swim in the creek.

Great getaway and camping ground.
Facilities good and clean.
Thunderbird park has many different adventures from tree top challange to thunder egg fossicking or take a dip in one of many crystal clear very refreshing rock pools some with running water falls .
Fantastic spot to spend a few nights under the stars.
They also have a restaurant,  rooms and glamping facilities.
Great fun for the family. Was a really hot day. Wear sun screen and a hat
Zip line is alot of fun!
It was so good I went there for my graduation and the location was so good and the food was amazing and we had such a great time
Was a great wedding venue. Lots of parking. Nice location 👌
Lovely activities!

Chris Arthy: he was absolutely brilliant man. My teams experience was great because of his customer service. He came to us and connected with us.  Everyone were great and across was outstanding. Our stay at thunder park was great due to his interaction and services. He mshould be rewarded for his best customer service.

Thank you Chris
Good location and place for short trip. A lot of activities for children and few for adults.

Beautiful venue for a wedding but also just somewhere to go as a couple or with the family.
Staff were amazing soooo super helpful.

Overall the park is great! Beautiful and very peaceful. We went for a work conference here and did the high ropes course, this was very challenging and exciting. The rooms were lovely, just a bit older. The service from the restaurant staff was the only thing I could say needs improvement, it's quite slow and staff are not very friendly, felt like a burden asking for a coffee, which also took very long. Regardless this won't deter me from going again to do the activities or stay.
Beautiful area with plenty to do, would have lived to spend longer. Facilities have been upgraded, with care taken to enhance the natural habitat which is stunning
Nice place, unfortunately we had a bad experience with staff when attempting to pay for mini golf. But im sure that was a one off.
Ronnie is one dynamite lady, she is incredibly accommodating and has made our wedding party feel so good this weekend and supported! Shout out Ronnie!

We went camping and it was really nice , there's a creek to swim but it's not a huge waterhole so may get packed with two families there. Amenities were really nice and close to cam site, very shady. Mini golf was good and was a nice location

We did the Tree Top challenge here and had a blast. For us, Lily made the experience. She kept us engaged from when she was instructing us on safety, to encouraging us as we attempted their difficult rope challenges. She was so confident and personable that we were feeling like friends, and were surprised to hear that she had only been there for less than a year. Definitely recommend checking this place out.
Thunder egg hunting was amazing

An amazing park for the family. Not overcrowded and plenty of activities to do.
Unfortunately we found all the activities to be quite overpriced though. Bird feeding is NOT what it’s made out to be in pics or by influencers. The young staff at the Jnr Treetops were all very friendly and helpful. Definitely recommend spending the day there (with pockets full).

Stayed a couple of nights in Frog Lodge as part of a wedding group — peaceful, scenic, and beautifully designed up on the hillside near the pickleball court. Staff were lovely and helpful, and the breakfast buffet was next level! After many average hotel buffets over the years, this one really stood out — fresh, delicious, and beautifully presented. Thank you for an awesome experience!
Got there for 1:30. Unfortunately the Zipline we wanted to do was stopped so ring ahead. Make sure what you want to do is open it.
We took the kids for Dig It.They enjoyed the day and the price was reasonable.

We just spent a lovely family long weekend here. I highly recommend for all the different activity options. We played mini golf, played with the mini diggers at dig it and fossicked for thundereggs. My 11yo girl and 13 yo boy loved every minute. Free wood and kindling for use in the firepit at our room and free family movie night were lovely touches. Most importantly for a young coeliac, they had Gluten-free options and separate fryer! 🎉 Can't forget to mention ALL the lovely, friendly staff, we did not have a lukewarm interaction with anyone. With special mention going to the lovely Kim who was warm, friendly and welcoming. So many more things to do, we're going to have to come back to do the other activities!

Stayed in a campervan on a tent site. Not very impressed with the amenities buildings, including the newer ones. Very tired and not too clean. These facilities were well below standard for similar priced parks.
Just did the eggs

It's a great adventure with my lil one , who has enjoyed every bit of but I feel the timings abit shorter of only 4 mins per ride but we will see you again next to dig it out cheers , we have a great time
One for the active family.

Kids had an absolute ball at the high ropes course. Was a little disappointed that there was no public seating/BBQ areas to have a picnic lunch while waiting. I understand that there is a cafe for people to enjoy but when the cost of the activities is quite high we wanted to do our own lunch. Thankfully there was a grassy patch of grass and we took some gas BBQs of our own. A really relaxed enjoyable afternoon.
Perfect family getaway! So many activities for the kids and plenty of space to relax. Staff were wonderful and welcoming - Thunderbird Park is a gem on the mountain!
Went to a fabulous wedding.
Great! 好玩，體驗活動非常有趣，住宿也很接近大自然！會想一來再來！
Amazing service, the camp ground was well maintained & the new amenities are absolutely stunning! Would highly recommend to come visit!

A must do adventure if ur in SEQLD. Very trained staff and are super observant. The obstacles as well as ziplines are very well designed and safe. Booking is easy if ur doing it in weekdays. Plenty of parking. Price more than truly justifies the fun, adventure, thrill and experience.

Day trip. I have been here before about 6 yrs ago. I would strongly recommend that the chain rope be moved over. Consider putting it in the middle of your walk away. Not over towards the edge? Its not easy to reach going up to dig site No 2. My girlfriend is 72, l'm 65, & a rock hound collecting rocks for years. We just managed to reach the rope. Its step she never been here. Sadly its a step rise, l be redoing the walk, with less gradient. Less slip.
Just a thought.
Was a great experience the staff are friendly and helpful
This is the coolest place ever. It's got everything from thunderegg mining to rope climbing to hikes & camping to facilitating weddings. Worth the visit
Great value for money, kids had a great day

What a great place for the kids!!

Digit and the staff were amazing and great for all ages ♡

Unfortunately the cafe was a little let down, served friends before us, little rude and to not serve coffees after lunch seemed odd.

$4.50 for a can of Pepsi. On what planet is this reasonable? Also, FYI- $20 for a bag of sand to find some coloured rocks. Against my better judgement, we spent $80… on sand. If you believe this is an unreasonable fee, I’m glad I saved you the drive up the mountain.

Fossicking was a great activity for the kids - very dusty and hot in the sun (make sure you take a hat and bottle of water).

A suggestion - it would be nice if a few staff were up in the dig site to help kids identify areas to dig or rocks that 'might' be worth taking down to be cut.

Whilst some parts of the park are looking a little tired it was still a fun day out overall.
앵무새가 우리나라 참새처럼 날아다녀요
We had a great time rock picking. However gloves for the kids would be a good idea for next time. Winter was great, but I imagine it would get very hot in summer.
Great for a day out with the kids
Great day out what our granddaughter  having a ball

A lovely day out, exploring the mining park.
Be wary, your child may see a big rock and decide that is the best rock ever and suddenly you're carry a 5kg rock in your bucket questioning your life choices.
You also wonder if you should maybe start exercising more as some fitness is required to traipse up the rocky incline
I’ve camped here many times, and one of my favourites. So many things to do, and activities at Tamborine Mountain literally around the corner. Feel confident going here, great place to camp!
Bello e divertente
Take some gardening gloves if you don't do a lot of physical work. To protect your hands while digging

We went glamping here it was amazing. The tents had all the modern touches, smart tv , ac/heating, BBQ, and outdoor shower, but the tent and shower showed signs of wear. Great camp-site vibes, and there are plenty of kookaburras and bush turkeys plus cooking on the BBQ at night with the led lights, then having a night shower with the stars was amazing. Wifi speeds were good too

Also a free EV charging point that doesn't seem to be advertised

So bad and poorly organised. The same line for cafe and put put. I waited an hour just to get clubs before even getting to put. The line went through the cafe and all the people in the cafe early food got star at our backsides.

We did not attend the hotel, we participated in the fossicking. Prepare to get dirty as you hunt for thunder eggs! Each family member can haul out up to 5kg of rock, and you can spend as long as you like there as long as you don't leave the site. Wear old clothes/shoes as the soil is clay: if it's been wet prepare to get dirty! If you're not a regular of manual labour bring gloves as after only a short amount of time we all were suffering some mean blisters. We followed staff instructions and each came out with a thunderegg, which cost $5-$20 to cut depending on their size. It's a great outdoor/novelty activity for the whole family. Allow about 1.5hours for your visit (mainly because  we didn't go crazy collecting too many rocks and it can be tiring work finding them and digging them out!). A must-do activity at Mt Tambourine that can easily be turned into a whole day thing as there is a high ropes course, mini golf and waterfall walk on site.
Best place for a family adventure ❤️ So much to do! 😍 Sooo much fun 😁
Great place to visit.  Staff are friendly.

Was here for Thundereggs fossicking. Bring child labour to work the mines. Pretty interesting considering you don't often find such activities especially if they are considered inhumane or outlawed. We spent sometime here working the kids to look for treasures but sadly they were inept and I did not bring a whip. Some of the staff here were great, especially the one who had to brief the child labourers. Really appreciate her enthusiasm seeing as she had to do this every hour and day. Overall good fun to spend a couple of hours away from brain rot and doom-scrolling.
Omg. Every activity ceases at 2.30pm. Drove all the way there only to be let down. Was hiking at the waterfalls prior to this. When we arrived every activity had ended. Such a let down

Absolutely amazing place!
Thunderbird Park is a hidden gem! From the moment we arrived, we were greeted by friendly and welcoming staff who made us feel right at home. The natural surroundings are stunning, and there’s no shortage of activities to keep the kids entertained all day—from adventure courses to mini-golf and gem fossicking. It’s the perfect spot for a family day out or even a weekend getaway. We’ll definitely be back!

Nik was our groups safety supervisor and was so fun from the start making jokes with us. Daniel joined us throughout the courses and both of them sat with us having conversations with us, and having a laugh. Daniel even raced one of us on the blue course which was really cool!
Thomas was the manager on who helped us at the counter and helped one of us right at the end with her fear.
They definitely knew what they were doing and it was so fun!

Overall, our family had a nice visit. However, one young woman in the restaurant staff were quite rude to my husband,  when he asked about his drink..creating tension at our family lunch. Not impressed.will come again for activities, but not for lunch.
Great fun. Feels like you in minecraft in real life.

If you're ever in the neighbourhood, I had the absolute best bowl of chips ever....crisp on the outside, piping hot and fluffy on the inside, chicken and regular salt. Will pay $10 again if I find myself in or near Tamborine again.
Also a lovely setting and meal for my cousin's wedding.
Had a great time today the staff where nice as and the courses where great
Beautiful location close to Tamborine town, in amongst massive gum trees, amazing facilities and great service. Thanks
Highly recommended.  Will definitely go back soon.
Went for 2 nights camping, and loved it. The facilities, restaurant and staff were all excellent.

Great place, however disappointed with the closing time for the food. It was 2:15pm on a Tuesday afternoon. We wanted to watch the bird feeding show at 2 and then have some late lunch. When we went up to order the server has said they had stopped serving hot food. I said oh it says kitchen doesn’t close until 3pm. The lady said it was dead so the manager closed it. A loss for your business for 5 people eating, we choose to have lunch elsewhere.

We had an amazing time at Dig It! It's a fantastic hands-on experience where kids can operate real excavators in a safe and fun environment. Perfect for lower primary school children – our child absolutely loved it and didn’t want to leave! Highly recommend for a unique and exciting day out.
It was $65 for 2 hours. You can try un-limited time.👍

Such a fun place. Great coffee and free bird feeding at 2pm
A lot of things to do here and camping onsite as well.
Expensive. Have to book into everything WELL in advance. But, kid had fun. That's all you can ask for
Fantastic place. A little pricey but we enjoyed our experience
always a great place to visit.
Very fun. Black course very advanced. Great day with the family
Great place for a group catch up, something for everyone to enjoy or to simply sit and chill.
They staff were super friendly, and the activities that they have there were super fun. My family and I loved it, and we will definitely be going back again.
Very busy, people camping cramped onto camp sites, suppose to be 1 car per site, camps set up on track suppose to be for vehicles. Definitely will not go back in holidays.
The girl at the mine was very good (to say the least). My son and I will definitely be back there in the near future.
The construction park is heaven for little boys. (And little boys in big men’s bodies… )

I haven't been here yet but I came here to read the reviews before we head over this weekend. I'm unable to comment on a comment or reply to the owners reply so had to make a separate post.
I was on the fence about heading over to the park (because I hate driving up that mountain). After I read the review and reply to the spilled yogurt incident, we will for sure be heading over this weekend. I can tell by your reply to this complaint that you run a family friendly park and you as an employer must respect your staff. This was the most honest and forthcoming reply to a 'complaint' I've even read. I feel that we will be looked after by your staff this weekend.
Camping good large powered sites

We had an amazing time looking for a thunder egg, managed to find this beautiful piece!
Would suggest getting there early with alot of time to spare.
Loved it ♥️
So much to do,look on website,fantastic place for familes
Excellent
Son went to a birthday party and did Laser Tag and he had a great time! Great little area there for the parents to chat and for food etc.

NOT FAMILY FRIENDLY! $50 CHARGE TO CHANGE DIRTY SHEETS. MOULD EVERYWHERE

We stayed in the “family tent”. $1000 we paid to stay in a mould covered tent

We decided to look past this and try to enjoy the stay. Unfortunately my 6month old son leaked through his nappy in the night. He was in the bed with me so the king bed got drenched in urine.

I thought no bother I will call into reception and see if they can change the sheets. The woman at reception agreed to this. But 10 mins later I get a call informing me there would be a $50 charge for this!!!!! After a rough night with 4 young kids my patience was thin. My mum went in  and spoke with them and for 15 min the manager would not reconsider the charge. She kept saying “it’s an expense to us”. Surely they would account for a change of sheets if an accident was to  occur in the $1000 we were charged for a 2 night stay. She was so rude and not considerate with what had happened. You would think for the price, they would be happy to help in a situation like this.

In the end my mum asked for the formal complaint process and with that she agreed to change the sheets.

However after bumping into the cleaner we found out the manager had told her to change the trundle bed not the king bed. Luckily she had some common sense (finally) and changed the king bed as she had noticed the sheets had been removed from it.

The tent
Mould covered, you could smell it as you walked in. The bbq was filthy. My boys accidentally smashed a glass there was nothing to clean it up with. The microwave, toaster, and kettle were in a spot that you can’t even plug them in. You have to move the appliance to use it. The views were stunning.

The resort
The play park closes at 4pm, the terrace cafe sells average, overpriced food, the other option is a fancy restaurant that is not suitable for kids. The walks are not pram friendly. Every activity costs extra. No pool!

After the interaction with the manager we wanted to just leave. But I had saved up for this trip and had wanted to come for over a year. So we decided to stay. But safe to say we won’t be returning. This place is not family friendly, although advertised to be. Such a shame as the place has so much potential.

One of my childhood memories I treasure with dad is him taking me here! I took my kids the other week and it was so much fun! Fair prices. Only thing I recommend is preparation for the heat! Hoping my kids hold the day in their memories 🥰
Difficult sights to get on. We had to be relocated. Very expensive considering all activities were a further cost.

We experienced the canyon zip lines today. Wow. So much fun, excellent scenery and our guides, Eli and Cameron were professional and fun. Everyone in the group felt super safe and confident flying above the tree tops. A must do!

Booked the Canyon Flyer to celebrate my birthday - What an experience. We completed the tour with Chloe and Ollie who were both fantastic guides. We had an amazing time and highly recommend this trip to everyone!
Thank you guys!

Friendly Atmosphere, But Reception Staff’s Lack of Empathy for Families Is Disappointing.

While the restaurant staff was incredibly friendly and accommodating for children, tasty cocktails, the buffet breakfast was lovely, and there were engaging activities for all ages, our stay was spoiled by a troubling experience. My 1.5-year-old daughter accidentally spilled yogurt on the reception carpeted floor while I was alone with my 3-year-old (my husband had stepped away to the bathroom). A guest nearby openly shook their head while 2-3 others stared, and this happened right in front of the reception desk. Despite the two reception staff being present, neither offered help—they simply watched as I struggled to clean the mess with baby wipes while managing two toddlers. No one from reception spoke to me during the incident. Later, when I called to express my frustration and humiliation, the manager told me that the staff member had called housekeeping, though I never heard or saw anyone arrive during the up to and what felt like five minutes I spent cleaning, with several guests standing less than a meter away, one even shaking her head. This experience left me feeling humiliated.

Additionally, while the room location was lovely, the room's cutlery and some bowls were dirty, and my husband’s steak at the restaurant was overcooked (he had requested medium-rare). Despite these issues, I still believe the restaurant is worth visiting for its atmosphere and offerings. However, based on this experience, I would advise exercising caution when it comes to the reception staff and their willingness to offer assistance or show empathy, particularly when it comes to accommodating children.

I’ve felt compelled to write positive reviews in the past for places I’ve visited but never a negative one until this experience.
Mill lodge awesome for families and friends....everything there just like home away from home 🤙

Went here for my sons birthday they went on the diggers and loved it staff were amazing also did the crystal panning kids loved it and staff were super friendly, also had dinner at the restaurant staff did a nice birthday plate for my son again staff were amazing 👏
Great cafe burgers are very good
Went digging for thunder eggs. Found a couple but don't underestimate the effort needed to dig these up.
A great family holiday location!

For my 10 years old daughter and I, this wasn't the first Tree top walk we have done.
So we saw more creative ones and fewer ones but this one was rather disappointing. It's basically all the same, such as walking on tight ropes, nets, and a few wobbly blanks. There are two green routes (easy) at the beginnings, then 2 purple ones (middle), 2 red ones ( should be more challenging than purple), 2 blues, and one black. Due to my daughters height, we could only do the green, purple, and red ones. She was very fast bored because of to little challenging and the repetitive options.
We spent around 3 hours in the park, not because it was difficult but rather to many people. We often had to wait on the platform for up to 20 minutes. Which kills the fun. My daughter also thought the courses were rather short, and on the map, the black and the blue looked very long.
Basically guaranteed to find thunder eggs. None were planted, all were naturally occurring within the rhyolite

We took our 4 year old to the junior ropes activity. It’s 100% one of the best things we’ve ever done. Initially I thought the $40 for a 4 year old was a bit over priced. But think you definitely get your moneys worth. The staff were a great mix of relaxed yet professional, they were great with all the kids. Will be back definitely
Great customer service  .Ben and Erin at the camp grounds maintenance were very professional and friendly they do a great job
We have spent an amazing weekend here, enjoying our time with the kids and a couple of friends. Cozy place, filled with nature, fun activities and peace & quiet. Exactly what all of us needed!
Had a great day at Thunderbird park, lots to do and see.

We went on the Zipliners - Canyon Flyer! It was great! The Instructors Harisson and Candy were absolute sports and new exactly what they were doing! It was 3 hours, up in the trees, and the views were incredible! Recommended, if you have a love for heights! Thank you

This is a one off visit for us. As a family of six we camped for my youngest son’s birthday so he could go to Digit. He enjoyed it which was great but everything else was just too expensive and the biggest surprise being that my husband had to pay $15 to watch the boys look for Thunder eggs. There really wasn’t much for them to do other than the paid activities which is why we won’t be back. Camping was $250 for 2 nights unpowered we had our hands in our pockets for activities all the time. Bird feeding you don’t get to feed the birds but just watch them squark around a bird feeder which entertained for a minute max and the swimming was hard to enjoy with all ages as it really is too tricky to navigate for the little ones.

Beautiful location on Mt Tambourine. Lots to do and the event venues are in an idyllic setting right on the running creek.  I didn't participate in the treetop challenge but  the younger ones did and it looked like a lot of fun.
Nice place with plenty to do for the kids.

Had a fantastic time. Ziplineing at Thunderbird Park.  Highly recommended for the adventurous. Unfortunately the pictures that they gave for free were not of very good quality, luckily we had taken some of our own.   Bring your own phone to get quality pictures.   I would definitely do it again!!!
Didn't stay here but attended a wedding

Recently our family had our second camping trip to Thunderbird Park after our first trip down from the sunny coast 3 years ago we made the trip down again for a weekend of fun and camping. We had an awesome time, the camping facilities were awesome and clean and tidy. But what really made our trip was the absolutely wonderful hospitality of the staff there. My daughter came back after a walk down to the rock pools with a tick on the back of her neck which as kiwis we hadn't experienced before, the staff were brilliant in helping my young daughter who was quite histerical in calmly removing it with all the necessary first aid equipment and very sweetly distracting her by chatting with her and keeping her calm with a lollipop. I was so impressed by their kindness and their patience with our children. We also ducked in and had breakfast at the buffet to finish off our trip with only half an hr left of breakfast they politely told wrs not to rush and even brought us out freshly made pancakes and chatted with our children. I was so impressed by their welcoming staff and easy going atmosphere. Will definitely be back again!

Thunderbird Park is one of our favourite places to visit. Been bringing the kids here for years! Been seeing lots of improvements over the years which is always wonderful to see. See you again next time!

Rude manager, not understanding, there also appears to be somebody living in the camping area and I was woken twice by he’s dog of leash in our campsite early hours of the morning. Also very noisy at night due to other campers

We were so impressed by Dig It. First time here visiting from overseas.

Our 5yr old enjoyed the experience.

My family and our friends stayed two nights at the campground. The kids was having so much fun trying out the diggers and playing around in the rock pools during the days and we spent the evenings doing bonfires and grilled some marshmallows. We can recommend this place!
We had the coziest weekend with our kids and friends here. Swimming in the creek, enjoying an ice cream in the restaurant and having cozy fires in the evening - our cups are filled!
Great place to spend time with the family and friends. We came here with our 3yo son and had the best time! We highly recommend.
Stayed at the vistas. Very clean neat and new. Restaurant has very nice atmosphere. Staff are friendly. Had a great time overall.
It is very fun
Amazing experience. Kids loved it.

This place offers many activities that cannot be done in one day. Treetop challenge is my favourite although it required specific height requirement and fitness to complete. And it will take up to half of the day minimum to complete all challenges. Further for the entire family, this is a nice calm and beautiful place for other activities such as dig it, crystal panning, mini golf, laser skirmish, rock pools, laser skirmish, pickleball, bird feeding, and cedar creek falls.
Excellent day out on the Ziplines with the kids and family 😊.

How can a place have the best restaurant and the worst amenities we've ever experienced in the same place? The amenities are old, need repairs to fix the shower heads and a thorough clean with bleach to kill the mould. They should really be demolished and new ones built if they're going to keep charging high rates like $70/night for a caravan site. However, the food and service at the restaurant was top class! Amazing share dishes in a beautiful venue with attentive friendly staff - such a contrast to the caravan park and atrocious amenities just outside. It seems as though they concentrate on weddings and activities and not camping/caravaners. It's in a good location for the attractions at Mt Tambourine like waterfalls, Skywalk, Gallery Walk village etc, if you can put up with terrible amenities.
The staff are very kind and helpful

Absolutely loved my time there today, was hard work but did go home with some pretty cool rocks.

They have a small gem shop as well which saves a lot of the digging, however they seem to cover a lot of them in resin instead of just polishing which sadly is part of the reason I did not buy anything.

Other than that it was great and I'll be going back.

Place is great but toilets are a REAL let down. We are regulars but won’t be coming back.
Last year we were told that the toilet block would be renovated… it was… but just the floor ?!?! So weird

The shower heads collapse on you and dribble DRIBBLE water.
So frustrating when you have three kids.

Fix this and you have a great site.
But they didn’t - bizarre

Update. I think this happens when all showers are on as I went back and the same shower was ok-ish
Maybe it’s water pressure but PLEASE sort it out.
The kids dig it was great although very structured around times even when the place is empty...
We came here to fossick for thunder eggs. It was a nice way to spend a couple of hours and we came away with some lovely finds.

From the moment we arrived the staff were very friendly and helpful.

We did Mini Golf and dug for Thundereggs.

The steep hill up to the diging area does have loose rocks, but take your time and you will get to both levels and make great memories with your little or big people. 😀

Fantastic adventure and exercise on the treetop challenge with my 8yo son
Had a ball and highly recommend the challenge

The staff are FANTASTIC! My daughter was so frightened of the first zip line. The staff offered to go with her over to the first point. However, in the end, she was just too scared and shaking They let us skip that point and start at the purple section. My husband isn't amazing with heights either, so after two hours of encouragement and trying we called it quits. They have given us a 12 month pass to come back when we think we can tackle it. They were so understanding with everything, there was no pressure at any point. So wonderful. Thank you so much everyone!

*We had passes given to our children through adrenaline. Dealing with adrenaline was a little frustrating because they don't know enough about the zip line/ park so definitely book through the park itself. Again they are amazing.

They talked a pretty good game about their 2pm bird feeding on their website. So we drove an hour from Brisbane to check it out. Sat at the area marked '2pm Bird Feeding' at 1:50pm. As did about four other families. No-one showed up. Birds started getting annoyed. We left disappointed at 2:20pm. Great waste of two hours on a Saturday.

The staff were lovely, personable and helpful. However, the powered camp-site we were allocated (B8) was right on the dual toilet block. The toilets were less than 2 metres from our camp pad and the waft and toilet traffic was unpleasant. The camp-site also had very little shade so it was not ideal. Looking around the immediate area, there were lots of lovely shady spots. I would highly recommend checking which spot you are allocated before you arrive to avoid this disappointment. Camp-site B8 and B6 are right on the toilets and septic tank respectively.
Other than that, we really enjoyed the activities Thunderbird Park offered.
Billy the bartender was awesome!! Excellent interpersonal skills, reception was lovely as well, definitely will be staying here!!

This place is amazing! Highly recommend for a weekend away or just a day trip. The zip line is wonderful for all ages, dig it is the most fun 2 hours a kid can have! I would recommend dog it for ages 3+ honestly there were teenagers and adults loving it!

Camping is great and so are the cabins.
The restaurant is a little too expensive in my opinion but there is a bar menu which we didn’t realise until later and i would recommend this over the restaurant menu. In the day there is a cafe outside and it’s okay but also too expensive for what it is.

Lovely relaxed vibe and very family friendly!
The Thunder Egg mine is an amazing experience for the whole family. Aiden, who helped us get ready was so friendly and enthusiastic which made it so much more enjoyable!
The service was outstanding and the staff friendly and approachable. Everything is cleaned regularly and we had a wonderful stay.
The common area was extremely clean and equipped well.
Good place for kids, everything was good except new camping ground. Hard to put peg into the ground. Not good for tent. Hard ground.
Brilliant glamping spot, fantastic restaurant,  very nice staff ( Sophie and others), only complaint was dogs barking at 6am, would have been nice to sleep in....

Great value for money experience for our 2 year old  - she loved getting on the diggers and playing with the digger toys when it all got to much (@digit).  Staff were friendly and lots of other experiences to explore at the same location to make a day of it
Was good fun this place 👍 highly recommend!

A great place to visit with kids. Our boys thoroughly enjoyed the gem panning and thunder egg fossicking - something very different for them to try.
Staff were happy to help and knowledgeable. A great famimy morning out .

This was an absolute highlight of our trip. with so many activities available, it's a great place to visit. We did the gem fossiking, and the amount of gems we found was fantastic. We also went mining for thunder eggs, which was great fun, although a bit of hard work. We also played mini golf on what is a reasonably challenging course. We saw plenty of other activities available but ran out of time. Def call in and check this place out
Amazing place with heaps of activities for kids. Could easily spend the while day there 😊

Awesome place to visit with kids, with plenty of different activities! We enjoyed the mini golf, crystal panning and fossicking, and came away with some unique pieces cut at the Rock Shop we pulled out of the ground ourselves. Great value, all within walking distance! And plenty more to do next time!
Excellent place for the kids to dig around and be kids. Fun for both big and little kids, and adults too. Lots to do for a day trip.
The kids had so much fun

Stayed several days at a powered van site with little kids. Couple of minor issues: Very difficult caravan parking manoeuvres due to a ditch running alongside the road. Reception + wifi nearly non-existant. Toilet/shower amenities are showing their age though apparently about to have a big overhaul. Apart from these, a great spot - beautiful, serene location. Flat, well draining sites. Great for kids. Lots of fun on-site activities (rock-hopping at the creek, mini golf, Dig It & tree tops junior)
The cabins are great, cosy.  Windows looking out onto cedar creek.  The kids loved fossicking for thunder eggs.

Such an awesome day out for kids and adults alike . We did the fossiking and the di
g it and while it was expensive the children loved the experience.  The staff were very professional and patience and showed the children how to work the machines if they were struggling and provided alot of encouragement.

100 percent recommended for a day out .

During a recent visit to Thunderbird Park, I had a deeply disappointing experience with the manager of The Rock Shop (I believe his name was Steve or Steven). His behaviour was shockingly unprofessional both towards guests and other junior staff members. While waiting in line to pay, we watched this man repeatedly treat these young staff members with utter disrespect, belittling and berating them in front of customers. This type of conduct is completely unacceptable and damaging to both the employees and the park's reputation. I was not surprised 😮 to find that multiple reviews echo similar sentiments about this manager's rude and hurtful behaviour. I have attached screenshots of these reviews to show how long this issue has been ongoing. Thunderbird Park needs to address this issue promptly to ensure a positive and supportive environment for both visitors and staff.
Great holiday with our grandsons. Excellent activities. Amentities are well past their use by date and need replacement.
Camped for two nights. Great for kids. Buffet breakfast was awesome.
Great management. Excellent Reception team. Many thanks for the business. 👍👍💪💪🙏🙏

Cut our stay short..no waterin the shower let alone hot water.camp staff running around in utes at 6 am like they were on a rally with kids in the park...camping is one thing but when you pay over $100 a night you expect water in your shower..all the on-site activities cost a arm and a leg..if you have a family with youg kids to entertain save you money...
Kids loved it
Plenty of family-friendly activities.
Great fun for the kids!!!

Super disappointing stay. As stated on the website curfew for camping is 10pm. We had nearby campers who were being unnecessarily loud at close to midnight. Called the reception number and couldn’t get through. Had to deal with this on our last night before an early wake-up and long drive ahead of us the following morning. Other than that stay was great, but wish customer service would go through with what it says on the website.
Plenty to do there
Absolutely LOVED this place ❤️

Spent the weekend here. Bit of a mixed review, the grounds are great, there’s a sign on the ammenities block to advise they are due for upgrade, I actually found the showers amazing! Great pressure and really hot, the camp kitchen has a jug and hot plate and power points (we cooked on our fire so didn’t need it but would be enough for camp cooking) and I saw the bathrooms being checked and cleaned regularly. I liked them but I can understand that for the price to camp there people would maybe have a higher expectation for the ammenities… but it’s camping… our site however, wasn’t great. We were in 626, large size site but the ground was really hard and no grass just a lot of gravel/rock/dirt. It rained the second night so it turned to mud and just made a big mess, a few large stumps/rocks. It’s really covered, so offers great shade, would be perfect in summer but doesn’t get a lot of direct sunlight so the mud wasn’t going anywhere. Love that it’s close to the water but allllllll day long all we could hear was the horn beeping from possibly the Digit activity, it wasn’t quiet either it was really annoying to be honest, just that repetitive beeping was quite irritating. We had the buffet breakfast on our last morning, loved it! Great continental selection and then the cooked breakfast had eggs cooked multiple ways, and a great price point, 6 of us was just over $100 and for a buffet I think that’s reasonable. The fires made it feel warm and inviting. There are birds everywhere they might even visit your table but it is quite open and I think it adds to the charm. The activities are quite expensive but they are owned separately, but the park does have daily bird feeding which is great for the whole family. Overall, I would definitely come back, but would book more in advance and request a different site.
Lovely pace for kids.kids had a great time at digit, treetop adventure and mini golf
We loved the Park. My kids spent hours on the ropes course and would have dug for Thunderbird eggs all day if we didn't need to leave.

We went to the mine today at thunderbird park with my daughter and son. The old man with white hair, not sure what his name was. Steve, was really angry and moody. His behaviour was disgusting. Felt depressing being there. He was having a go at this kid and that kid was so nice and lovely. Think this kids name waa Bradley. I may come back because of Bradley but that Steve guy really needs to keep his emotions at home. It was super awkward.
Camping was good. Nice big sites.
Treetop challenge was awesome!
Great visit for both children and adults.

We would like to personally thank Ben , Ben has gone above & beyond helping us at Christmas try save our dog, he took time out of his busy day to assist us,  we had the opportunity to meet Ben and personally thank him for his help last weekend and we were so pleased!
Awesome service, that's our 3rd stay there and we will be back!!
Lots of fun
They changed the way to book recently, and it is so useless make people so annoying
Great conference venue.   Excellent service and friendly staff.   Great food and location.   Highly recommended.
We camped here for our little mans birthday so we could go to Dig it. Was an awesome experience. Kids loved the whole stay. Beautiful grounds and surrounds

On my weekend stay I stayed at the Platypus accommodation. The room was clean and tidy. The light on outside my room all night less pleasing especially given there was no lighting in the car park. Someone with very little ethics took my car spot. When reported the solution whilst sympathetic in their voice was to park in the public car park. They stayed in my spot all night. I also noted another person had their spot taken so they parked in someone else’s. I did not and walked all the way back uphill from the public car park.
My son and family stayed in the camping site whilst we enjoyed our stay.
The ammenities were foul the caravan pads very in uneven and deadly for small children with broken bitumen all over the place my grandchildren walked away with bloody knees and many bruises.  I believe you need to get an arborist out as there a some huge widow makers hanging in the trees waiting to fall. The ground and gardens  were well maintained and activities were great the one star is for the camping site ammenities and the very annoying  parking issue.

stayed in the camp ground over the weekend.

nice grounds, good size camp site.
toilets definitely need a clean. was very disappointed that there was no hotwater.

price is a little in the high side so won't be back oftern
Beautiful grounds. Amenities are in desperate need of care they smell like someone's died in there

Great site, well appointed showers and bathrooms (no hot water though).
Also, the site has a 10pm noise curfew, but a wedding was being held that we could easily hear past 11pm.
Also the map for getting to the camp plot was not correct.
Absolutely perfect in every aspect. The place is maintained beautifully and there is so much to do.

Thunderbird Park on Tamborine Mountain was fantastic. The Dig-It experience was a hit with my kid. The park's beautiful natural surroundings and friendly staff made our visit truly memorable. It's a must-visit for families looking for a day of adventure and exploration in nature.
Brilliant!! We had an amazing camping stay! The lead up was easy and lots of communication from your staff, they were always so happy to help on the phone when we had questions too.
Amazing as usual!?!
Huge fun for both adults and kids! Probably best designed for 4 and up, who absolutely loved the crystal panning, bird feeding show and, of course, Dig It!

We were served by Richard and he went above and beyond to help us in our moment of need. He was friendly, professional and nothing was too much for him. This gave us a great experience in thunderbird park, we will visit again and stay!

Took our son to Dig it which was fantastic and Thunderbird Park its self is a great day trip and location with lots of activities. The terrace kiosk was our only let down, with 2 coffee orders taking 25 mins to be made. For a weekend it wasn't busy when we ordered, I would hate to think what the wait time would have been if it was busy.
Most things under construction at the moment, website does not work properly, wifi is very poor, no proper kitchen, so far from my experience only ok for glamping.
Staff were excellent as was the food

This place was wonderful enjoy the serenity immensely,the only criticism I have is ability to actually find your site but staff were very helpful to physically show me I certainly recommend staying here
Great place for a nature retreat with a lovely high end restaurant

a great place for a weekend trip - amazing spot. really enjoyed our stay in a tent. lovely that fireplaces are at each spot. bathrooms/kitchens could need a little improvement but are fine if its not too busy.definitely coming back
Wonderful bush camping. We had brought our caravan. Very friendly staff. The toilet block is a bit old now and will be replaced this year, however it was cleaned multiple times during the day.

I'd give 3.5 stars.

Camp sites can be pretty small, especially if you need to avoid obtacles.  Site initially assigned was a giant puddle, so we asked to be swapped to one we could actually set up at, and the check in staff was happy to do so.

My biggest concern is the large gap / gully between many of the sites and the road and amenities block.  Its quite difficult to jump over it or quite a long way to walk around, and it has bits of pipe and such sticking out. Its frankly unsafe and hope that some bridges or other solution is put in place soon.

In the amenities block one of the flat top BBQs is out of action and the 'Do not activate' tag is dated long ago.  A small electric frypan has been provided as a replacement.

They also missed the 2pm bird feeding when we went, which my son was pretty bummed about and as we were staying a single night we weren't able to do it another day.

There is a LOT to do for kids, for additional cost such as mini golf, high ropes, laser skirmish, digging, panning for gems and fossicking.
Good clean fun.The accommodation was very good for the price.
No advertised wifi internet except at the reception/restaurant area?

To think you could trust and be safe in a business’s site where they will help and be of service to those who paid to be there if horrifying events were to happen WRONG!
LACK OF EMPATHY for families. I stand with ALL those that experienced awful events during Christmas and they don’t want to refund money. They put there business before helping those who are in need. Appalling acts of service to those who cried out for help. Yes everything looks good but when in need of help or experiencing any bad events they will ignore you as human beings, slap you on the wrist with a credit and move on. DID NOT CARE FOR WHAT WE WENT THROUGH JUST ANOTHER MONEY HUNGRY COMPANY!

While Mt Tambourine is a picturesque place, the prices charged at this place completely ruin the experience. We stayed in the caravan area, $70p/n is absolute top dollar and you would at least expect a concrete slab for that….. nope, no slab, no grey water, no dump point, amenities are disgusting at best. I would not recommend staying at Thunderbird Park. Definitely day trip to the area or stay somewhere that has prices that match the quality of venue.
We have been here for a day. Whole family enjoyed. So many things to do for kids and adults. Zip line, mini golf, horse riding, rainbow lorikeet feeding.
Daughter and son in law enjoyed the fossicking
Staff are fantastic.  The park is very child friendly and the park is like visiting a North Queensland rain forest.

Love camping here. The sites next to the creek are perfect for dogs and kids to explore along the rocks & swim.
Love the toilet and kitchen area.

Perfect for a quick weekend get away to nature.

Enjoyed my camping experience here! Recommend choosing your spot in advance when booking. Conveniently, ice and firewood are available at the reception. Cooler temperatures than Gold Coast, and the bonus of a provided fire pit.
Looks lovely. Only called in briefly

I went to watch the birds feed  but ended up going around for a look..Great place for families and the adventurous..Had a ice cream at kiosk surrounded by all the lovely birds..The rain forest resturant and bar looked very nice..
A very fun place to bring your family. parking and other amenities on site. There is also a mine tour available.  Be careful as it gets slippery when it raines it's pretty hard to walk to the mine.

Worst place I visited in QLD. Cafe is terrible came for lunch. Food overpriced. Shop closing at 11:30am. Sandwiches soggy and when asked for a new one the chef responds. “We are closed. What do you want me to do?” Asked for napkins for the kids to wipe hands. Again got told “we are closed”  Don’t waste your time here. Spend your money elsewhere.
We camped here and really enjoyed the location and the facilities. Highly recommended.

Another 1 Star review  for the Christmas day events that my family experienced.
Feel free to reply to say "we should not have opened to begin with" as hindsight is 20/20 all well and good when take our money for services that could not be provided.

I'm not saying this place is a bad, it most likely very good when all things work as planned. But when something goes wrong they will run and hide behind everything they possibly can to keep your money. (Credit for 24month, no REFUNDS)

Given the owner is has a marketing background, they only care to get new customers in the door, but once the money is in their hands they wont return it. I wouldn't be recommending this place to any one just on their poor management and lack of customer service., and refusing to have any apathy for the customers and their individual circumstances. One Size fits all doesn't always work.

Stayed at the glamping tents with family from Japan on New Years Eve. Tent was fabulous, amenities were great. Had a blast until the rain started coming down at 10:30pm and it’s well documented what followed…. New Years Day we checked out after negotiating various torrents just to get to the lobby, as the communications were down, the staff were more than helpful in trying to give us the best advice possible to help us get home safely.  We tried to get off the mountain with no luck.  We were stranded and had nowhere to go, so we turned back to the Thunderbird Park and all the staff welcomed us back with open arms, offered us free accomodation and food to help us through this disaster.  Special thanks to Kaye who was just an angel, keeping our morales up and updated us with the latest news.  Thanks again for an unforgettable New Years!
Fun day, lots of activities for kids.

Terrible management
No disaster recovery planning, evacuation information.
Was using the site during the thunderstorms on Christmas night with only 2 onsite staff helping reliance on ses
Refusal of refunds (credit only)
No communications from management after the event.
Hides behind disaster event to avoid responsibility.
Exceptionally disappointing

Wifi is terrible could add 1 booster and the whole place would be fine and makes a coffee then 10 minutes later brings out cold drinks
Forgot to say the rooms had water in the carpet smelt terrible
We didn't stay at the park overnight but took my grandson for the dig it ut park and he absolutely loved it.  Going to come here earlier next time to fit in the rest of the features of the park
Love it I love the rock hunting but it is a bit of a walk

This is a spectacular site with nature abound and a crystal clear creek running through it with an array of adventure and nature activities for all ages. With camping, awesome new glamping sites, renovated hotel rooms and cabins, and creek side villas to choose your style of accommodation with wonderful conference facilities, recreation rooms, cafe, restaurant and corporate meals to boot!  The owners Bob and Judi and all dedicated staff take great pride in their services and upkeep of the premises. I have been going to Thunderbird Park and Cedar Creek Lodges for over a decade and have witnessed incredible growth, renovations and upkeep of the premises as well as maintaining a diligent team of professionals and staff during Covid lockdowns. I feel saddened to read of  the 1 star ratings. This was a once in a lifetime, unexpected tornado in a natural environment where there are imminent risks of camping under trees. The unique events on Christmas Eve are reflective of the site being knowingly closed and was equal to the massive destruction felt across Queensland. Everyone was underprepared and did their best to get through that awful event:
Let’s band together in times like this, exercise tolerance, kindness and apathy for all involved. The owners and staff are busting and working tirelessly to recover from this event, at peak season and only a distant  throw from Covid closures!  I find these comments very unAustralian and lacking compassion and deeper understanding and acknowledgement of all
circumstances for the sole and unique event.

Cedar Creek Lodges and Thunderbird Park- I rate you 5 Stars! And wish you a speedy recovery.

What a fabulous place! So much to do for the kids. We went specifically for mining and it was fantastic. Pay once and you can stay the whole day. It got a 9.5/10 for a range of kids from 7-14 boys and girls. A really fantastic Xmas gift for all the cousins. Food available, heaps of other activities and camping too!

Had booked a two night stay in the vistas on 27th and 28th december. We were advised we couldnt check in on 27th due to the storm damage however later advised we could stay our second night on the 28th. When greeted we were advised we can either spend our credit of $416 from the first night within their facilities or credit it for 24 months, no refund was allowed and told that the ACCC had confirmed they could refuse a refund. We said we wouldnt be accepting this. We went to vista room 1 and immediately found the floor soaking wet. Afraid to raise an issue after being aggressively told no refund, however couldnt bare to walk on wet floors for our stay. Our neighbour in vista 2 also had wet floors. Asked if there was another suitable room available, staff were surprised at the wet floor and moved to vista 5. The floors were still very damp, but not as bad as number 1 so we decided to not pick a fight and grin and bare it. Many others we passed into reception were also unhappy with the service and responses on the refund situation. We understand you have been affected by the storm and are trying to mitigate your loss, but the damage you have done to your reputation far outweighs the immediate financial loss of opening your doors when your vistas were soaking wet and not ready and secondly being so aggressive in withholding our money on a service you were unable to deliver. No additional compensation or enticements were offerred. Won't be back and won't be recommending thunderbird park or cedar creek lodges to anyone. Very dissapointing.

Had a fabulous stay with amazing service and staff, stay ended with a flash flooding weather event and we were VERY WELL looked after,  the staff were absolutely stellar considering they only had a few on that had made it through.
Cannot express highly enough how fantastic we were treated and cared for and how grateful we are to ALL the staff at Thunderbird 👌👌👌
Loved fossicking for thundereggs and gemstones. Boys loved the Dig it zone, being able to operate heavy machinery. Has a cool off area. Great value all around.
Bang average place for the price sites are so poorly marked and overcrowded everything was over priced definitely dont recommend to anyone

This is a very nice location and I am happy that I stayed here. That is where happiness ends, on the website they stated that they will have amenities upgraded in 2023, all the signs say it will be in 2024. They have rain showers in the shower blocks without the water pressure to use it also the drainage is non existent you will have a bath whilst having a shower. They will make sure that they don't miss a penny charging $4.50 for a can of coke. And obviously laundry and any other amenities is top dollar. Lastly anyone can have a fire which I love but when you have people that just smother and produce lots of smoke for all the rest of the users it takes away from the clean crisp mountain air. Oh lastly be prepared for lats of generators.

Awesome customer service, fantastic activities will return in the future.
Went to the park only as a day trip so didnt require accommodation

I don't agree with your comments in other people's reviews.We stayed at site 618 for three nights from the 23rd.
On the night of the 25th, trees suddenly broken by the typhoon hit our tent and the campsite was hell.
The sight of the typhoon passing by was terrible.
Most of the gazebos were crumpled like paper and trees were blocking all roads.
There was a power outage and I couldn't see anything.
There were no emergency lights or an emergency shelter anywhere, and no one was there to guide us.
I couldn't even go to the bathroom in the dark, and I spent a terrible night in a broken tent.
The morning scene was even more terrible, and I tried to get out of there, but the staff didn't help.
He just said sorry and left.
Our car was blocked by trees, so we made a way out of it, clearing out large stones and trees on other sites.
We managed to escape and we expected someone to guide us to the reception, but there was only water and oranges, no guidance.
We paid for one more day, but we had no choice but to come out.
The bathroom was terrible, and there was no electricity, no Internet.
We left the tent to repair today, but they couldn't fix it .
We lost tents, gazebo and many supplies and even suffered trauma.
So don't lie that Thunderbird Park didn't suffer any damage.
I  hope you to put more effort into safety.
Now, rather than trying to advertise for new customer,  try to ask the affected customers how they are doing and refund the unused accommodation fee.

We were staying in tents right when a tornado type storm happened. There was no emergency plan/ safety plan given whatsoever leaving us having to find safety on our own in the most dangerous conditions with MASSIVE tree branches/trees falling down every second, mind you we had about 7 children under 10 with us having to run and find somewhere safe enough to camp out till we could leave. Very traumatic experience not much help or guidance was given the day after it hit, tents ripped to shreds, gazebos with fallen branches on them seemed like the thunderbird park staff were prepared as we did see sandbags left along the doors of their building? looks like they were prepared for something that they did not warn any of the campers about. Also bathrooms were pretty gross.

Not liking this place for couple for the couple of reason
1) Our Tent site was occupied by other family, we were waited for couple of hours cant able to set-up our tent because of that, we complained couple of time , provide other options but they cant help us ..ultimately we need to do our own arrangements
2) Bathrooms & Toilets : zero cleanliness.
3) we had thunderstorm and you cant even find single person on site.
it is poor managed caravan park. lack of emergency plan. my car got crashed by fallen tree trunk.

I visited for the Treetop Challenge, where the staff were very encouraging and friendly! Even offered to take some videos of us up on the course. I took a walk around the park after, which has some lovely scenery and small trails. It is a fantastic place to spend the day.
We did the Canyon Flyer, was amazing! Gabby and Sam were excellent guides
Took my kid to dig it and he had a great time, Staff super helpful too.

Absolutely beat all the theme parks in Gold Coast in every way, most favourite and exiting attraction on our stay.
We did the guided Zip line with staff Eddie and Calum who both interacted with the whole group to make the day a completely amazing fun experience - they both have personalities that reduce any fear or anxiety of heights and make everyone laugh and enjoy the whole 3 hours
Next holiday we will stay at Thunderbird Park for accommodation to capture the whole experience and beautiful surroundings
Only regret is we didn’t have more time to explore the many attractions and activities available at Thunderbird Park - all ages and fitness/fear  levels will love this
Shel 🌈🦋

2 star based on food rating. avoid their chicken caesar that is sold as a grilled chicken salad

Staff are friendly enough and great place to go for the day

Didn't stay the night but took my little one to the crystal panning activity. Service was great . It was $15 for a bag of sand mixed with so many  crystals .
The panning piece was so easy and my little one had a blast ! It's a fun short activity to do after the bird feeding at 2:30pm .
Called to have a look,walked around, a beautiful place,A very popular Park,come holiday time .
One of the best family camping area near to Brisbane City
Good fun for the kids
Great minigolf
Went up for the day and had a lot of fun. The Gem Mining was a hit with the kids.
Enjoyable experience

The restaurant offers a great dining experience, with staff who are extremely friendly and helpful. The food sets a high standard, with great tasting meals, and a wide range of wines and liqueurs.

There are a few activities nearby, such as mini golf, which cater to different playing abilities, as well as a lovely tree top course.
First time back in over ten years and the manager Scott is a legend. Was very informative and interesting to listen to. Can't wait to come back

Just visited with my young family and had a fantastic time! Participated in Dig it, tree tops Jnr & thunder egg fossicking.  All activities were thoroughly enjoyed and we were very impressed with the knowledge and enthusiasm of the staff involved in those activities.
Good fun for all age family/s.

We did hours of fossiking at the Thunderbird park and came out with a bunch of cool finds, but by far the best part was the amazing and funny interactions we had with aidan he was so energetic and welcoming and made the whole day more fun.
We did the Canon zip line and it was amazing! Our Tour guides Georgie and Ayla were fantastic 👏

Amazing for families,kids loved it the tree top challenge its a must do activity,
Thouggt thw dig it was a bit pricey they should have a package for all activities.

Toilets arent the beat but I know there is an upgrade coming soon for 2024

Id definitely go again
Stopped for a late lunch, food was simple (I had an Angus burger) but top rate in flavour and presentation. Coffee, well I was very happy, barista knows their job.

No matter what review you give the owner after staying she will attack you instead of taking comments onboard.  Several people I know would not stay here again.  Nasty emails after the fact from the owner and manager "oh we were going to give you a credit until you gave us a bad review"  pathetic !!!  Yes we did stay here and of course she will say it never happened and has a short memory !!!

Awesome spot with an on-site dig site to find prehistoric “eggs” and sift for “gem” stones.
Mini golf and laser tag was great too. Next time we might try the high ropes.

Short stay in passing, but met all my needs.
Thanks.

Regarding the camping/caravan sites:

Honestly, if you can avoid staying here, I would.

For the money you are paying, it is nowhere near worth it. I would expect to pay less than half for the facilities and service provided.

I paid $70 for a powered van site. The site itself was okay, flat enough for me to set up my rooftop tent.
The power box was nowhere near the flat part of the site, and was to be shared between 4 sites it looked like. Two of the sockets were damaged and I was reluctant to use the other socket(the 4th socket was in use). There wasn't water for our site, and the park suggests you use bottled water anyway.

We were lucky enough to stay on a Friday night and there were some lovely guests staying at the Golden lodge that decided it would be a great idea to stay up till 230am playing music and talking loudly amongst themselves. That kinda summed up the stay.

Yes, the amenities are run down, and not very big considering the size of the park. I had a good shower in the males. My partner had a dodgy door and poor water pressure in the females.

Reception was poor, Optus had 1-2 bars where we stayed. Telstra had 1-2 bars as well. The place has WiFi, but it doesn't reach the campground, lol. I have no idea where it extends to, but nowhere that I walked around to.

All in all, probably the worst camp site I've stayed at during my month trip of QLD, sorely disappointed.

UPDATE:
The park apologised for the noise and offered us a free night stay. We graciously accepted since it fit in with our plans. The new site was slightly less level but it had power.
Our sleep was better. This time there was another party blasting music but it stopped at midnight instead of 230am. At this rate, if we stayed another night it might be quiet by a reasonable time.

Took my 4 year old daughter here to complete the junior tree top course. The staff were great, friendly and helpful. They had a lot of kids to manage and did a great job. My daughter had the best time. Definitely recommend!
We did the canyon flyer tour with Eddie and Sam and had the BEST time. It was a highlight of our family trip. Highly recommend. I’m scared of heights but still loved this!

This 2 points goes to the fact that our glamping tent only initially was set up for 2- rather than the 3 we asked for. There was a humidifier in room with hot air and stinking hot day. So hot to walk into room that is all zipped up. Bathroom door came off hinge and mum was stuck in bathroom until myself and son fixed up door. Realised by day 2 that the fridge was not plugged in and out food all hot. Poured rain 2nd day and TV wasn’t working . They did though get a lovely technician in day 2 to fix it up. Had wedding on both nights and the music and DJ was pumping till 11pm. You do need to pay for everything there and could at least be discounts when you are a staying guest! There was no drinking running water first day( they were testing water for bacteria)  and even so it said - bottles of water in room - there was none - they said they stopped doing this.
Tents too close to each other !!

Positives- great cleaning staff. Lovely TV technician. Excellent Breakfast!  Loved the bathroom. Fire place and shed to get more wood.

I believe they need to stop expanding and really focus on what you have and improve it .
Pity you built the tents so close to each other .

We are staying at thunderbird park for the last few days and this place is amazing the staff were very accommodating when we arrived and I had booked a site that just wouldnt work with our camper trailer the lady and manager on the desk were quick and happy to try and move us to more suitable spot that worked for us without any hassle.
Toilet blocks were always cleaned the staff were there all the time staying ontop. Activities were amazing for my 6year old I think the prices for the Activities are reasonable and if your coming here for a hoilday or daytrip to budget for that.
We will be back for sure.

I recently had the pleasure of taking my daughter to Thunderbird Park.  We were lucky enough to have Ryan and Jake as our guides for the Canyon Flyer, and wow, what a great adventure that was.

It is well worth the money, and Ryan and Jake make the whole adventure entertaining.  In our group were some very nervous flyers to people who just loved the entire experience, and they were excellent at catering to everyone.

Thank you, Ryan and Jake, for making it a wonderful birthday for my 15-year-old daughter.
Horrible camp sites!!! We were on display for the whole adventure park to see, people attending the park constantly walking through our camp site 🙄😕

Beautiful location amongst the trees with space to find a quiet spot. Disability access and services are limited with most pathways gravel and uneven.
Coffee was good but the service was slow and not enough space for the amount of people wanting to use the cafe. Good activities for children of all ages, even the kids a heart. Car parking would be an issue during peak times.
We stopped in and had a game of Mini Golf. Will definitely be back next time we are on the goldcoast to do some other activities. The place is stunning and well maintained.

Had a great day out with my grandkids they love it .they when mining and panning we walk around the park lots to see .All the staff were lovely and friendly. The coffee and food at the kiosk was real good too .  Thank for a great day

We were camping. The sites were mostly a good size. Amenities were close but old.
The activities available were great. The location of the park was great.
However some of the sites we booked 8 months ago were changed. As we were a large family group, we deliberately booked very early and booked sites near each other. These were changed at the last moment. I don’t understand how or why that should happen!!!!
It is unsatisfactory to do that to us when we booked and paid so much in advance.
Lots of activities!!

Took our 3 boys here for the digit activity based on all the reviews, however we were a little disappointed. The session time was 2hrs, however due to the sessions being packed with as many kids a possible you only had on turn on each machine. This means your child only gets 28mins of machine I total and the rest of the 1.5hrs and more, they are standing around waiting. This would be a fabulous activity for the kids if they didn't try to make as much money as possible and overfill the sessions. At one station only 1 of my sons had time to complete the full activity before we got told to more on.
I wouldn't recommend due to the price, and the wait times. If they committed to having smaller groups and more time on the machines, this would be a fabulous place to attend, but not in its correct form.

5 stars for wedding venue.
Rooms (frog lodge 6) not so great, clean and cleaning staff do an AMAZING JOB but the rooms really need work. Leak in the wall that drips ALL NIGHT, shower leaks (was told this at check in)  Not to mention we had booked months in advance, being there to officiate the wedding of dear friends that had already paid THOUSANDS of dollars  in venue, cabins and rooms for the week leading up to the day and the night of and was refused early check in of 30 mins (1.30pm) to get ready for a 3pm wedding start. Check in at 2pm, (50% of the room rate for an extra 30mins). Especially disappointing when at time of booking was told we could have the extra 30 mins  as we would have to arrive at 830am the day of to assist with other duties. Service needs work especially at the Cafe and restaurant. Arrived for breakfast at 9am paid $75 for 2 people and then had to pay for coffee and waited 45mins for 1 cuppa that didn't arrive until asked as to when it would be served. Ended up with take away coffee after breakfast.   Needs more staff for busy periods so your not waiting 25mins outside and 45mins inside for a cup of coffee. Wait times are really ridiculous considering the fees and pricing charged. Venue for the wedding 5 stars, although be aware staff are NOT quick to rectify any issues or noise in the carpark when there is a ceremony in progress.  Celebrant was ready to leave to rectify a car alarm that continued to go off every 5 or so minutes for the 45min ceremony which then had to run 30mins over due to this. Video grapher also not impressed.
Went there to fossick and knowing nothing. Followed the advice given at the beginning and we came home with some lovely thunder eggs. Will be going back for sure.
I had alot of fun learning how to find Thunder eggs. My grandson was so excited to see what we had dug up. I woulf like to do so other activities. We didn't stay it was just a day trip.

Thought we would give this place a try so we camped for 3 nights here. Probably never again. Total dust pit and the amount of showers (2) and toilets for amount of campers is always a wait not to mention any toilet paper by the evening.
