Anything for the kids costs a fortune here and for the price of a camp-site each night is expensive and a big 4 is much better value if camping with kids. Won't be recommending this place.

Edit after reply:  For what you pay it's not worth it. Also you have very unhappy over worked staff by the sounds of the conversation the whole Cafe area heard while waiting for coffee. Fighting over who was going to bring it to the owners attention about not having enough fridge space for the drinks that are sold. (Kinda like the showers and toilet situation, for the amount of guests)
This is not a park to stay if you have more than 1 child ( I have 3) . Rock pools are free everywhere and paying for a jumping castle is ridiculous. We payed for the 3 kids to dig and then had to pay $15 an adult to watch them. Then pay to get them cut open. It never ends here $$$ . I'm just letting families know what they are in for if booking here. I think you need to take some of these comments on board.
Activities at the park were excellent, we did the adventure tree top course and dig it and had a fabulous time.

Excellent service and friendly staff.
1000% for staff knowledge and charisma. Great team environment 👏
Made 1st time adventure an easy and confident one for all sizes 😀
Great job guys you rock and all deserve a pay rise for the work you do 😉

Great place for day trips for any adventurous adults and kids. Took our two young kids (below primary age) to Dig It. Really cool, kids under 5 have to be on a parent/guardians lap to operate the excavators but really well run, helpful staff and was really safe. Good price too, only pay for the kids, accompanying adults go free. My wife and I previously did the treetop challenge which was great fun too. Yet to explore the other activities here but will in future.
Glamping tents are great! Really well set up and comfortable.
Our family had an awesome day, the children loved driving the machines and fossiking. The snacks that we had were delicious and it was great to get gluten free options.
Was good. Very expensive, accom well priced just everything to do in park is expensive. I get it, got to make the dollars. But adds up
Excellent experience for campers

We had a great time with our grand sons at Dig It. Friendly staff and fun activities for the littlies. The kids had plenty of time to enjoy all the activities, it seems issues with earlier reviews have been sorted. Well worth the drive up from the Coast.
My neice had the best time ever. I love that everything is onsite and we don't need to leave at all. Nice and relaxing.

We had a amazing time with great accommodation, activities, location.  We had 25 of our family attend and everyone had a great time.  The minor lodge for our function was just perfect with fantastic food.
What a great place to come for anyone. With so many different activities to choose from there is something for everyone.

I won't be coming back, all of your customer service team is useless or so rude.

I work in customer service myself and I sure as hell wouldn't have a job if I treated customers the way I have been treated.

I don't know how anyone keeps their job here.

I've had to complain about the amenties being cleaned, the last time they were cleaned was 5 days ago according to the cleaning roster, disgusting!

I would rate a 0 on everything to be honest, I won't be back.

- Severe lack of amenities for the number of people using them.
- 2 showers in the block, with only 1 shower able to work at a time due to terrible water pressure. If one shower was already in use, you could only get a small dribble out of the other shower.
- Hot water stopped halfway through the kids shower.
- Amenities very dirty. Cleaning schedule shows full cleans once a week and spot cleans when needed however the showers consistently had  gravel/mud on the floor and in the mats and toilets smelled with dirty floors and walls.
- The amenities are certainly showing their age, I've been reading other reviews that they were to be scheduled to be replaced over a year ago. We received an email stating they were to be replaced later this year, on the blocks themselves they state 2024.
- Reception staff had no idea about park activities e.g. where to find them in the park, how to book, cost etc. Their advice was to look on the website for further information however phone service was rare.
- Everything was very run down, money made is obviously pocketed and nothing much put back into the park.

For a park of this size and popularity I certainly expected a lot more professionalism and a higher standard but I guess their well known name gets visitors coming along regardless of the disappointments.

On a positive note, the kids had a ball playing in the creek, rock pools and wading pools.

Well, the overall best tree top ropes course around.
Great tall trees with plenty of shade cover, extra safety features with unlockable carabiners.
Staff were very helpful and alert.
Camping is next level with great sites and plenty of wild animals like birds, roos, lizards, and those wild kids running wild, haha
I advise you to book the ropes course in advance online and fill out a waiver form so that when you arrive to check in, it's just as easy to give your booking name and go around the side to wait to be call inside to rig up.
Make sure to take some kind of water bottle or camel pack as your kids will ask to go all the back to the car or the shop to get water, and if you're not fit, then them return walk uphill on stairs will drain your soul haha.
Just go and enjoy as I trust you your kids will sleep well that night

Very nice place for a group of friends and families❤️ Toilets and showers are been cleaned 5 times by their staff. we really appreciate and enjoyed our stay, we will definitely come back and stay more longer days🥰

5 stars fall less. Amazing staff with tremendous support. They are so patient with the practice session especially for beginners who are nervous. I demanded this gift for my 40th Birthday from hubby and kids. It's worth it and feel very proud for asking 🤩😜🙌🌷.  I and my younger one we both were nervous in the beginning but achieved all the medium challenges with great enthusiasm and fun. I was chanting all the God's and Goddesses name in the world before and during the beginning of the first challenge 🤣☺️🌷.
You have water fountains in-between the challenges where you can take a break and hydrate yourself.
We reached an hour early and to kill the time we bought mini golf session. This was also firs experience  for us as a family game. This is a bonus gift for me 😀🎂.
Your food is not allowed inside the campus but Cafe should serve your purpose.
If you are adventurous or want to try something new please visit atleast once.

The place including the open spaces, lawns, the caravan and camping areas are great. The people manning are very helpful and friendly. I was lucky to meet Jamie and Coco. But for Jamie, I would have to sleep in the car after arriving late from Sydney, I just couldn't set up the tent by myself, thank you Jamie kindly, hope our paths cross somewhere sometime.

The place has a suttle spiritual ambience as well, great place for contemplation and introspection, not to forget meditation. The chirping of birds, the bright stars enhances it all.

We did the Tree top Challenge and it was super fun but a little scary to be honest. I would say some of the courses may be harder for some younger kids as a teen I even found it hard and do have to give a big push on the zip lines as I personally got stuck twice on them just don't panic 😰. The people there where very friendly and helpful and the got some ice creams and drinks at the kiosk which where very expensive. Overall I recommend this place!

Recently did the Canyon Flyer Zipline.   Amazing experience and would highly recommend if you are adventure minded.  The scenery was incredible and we even got to an Aussie critter!
Blair and Nikki, our lovely guides were so much fun and kept us safe while challenging our comfort zones.   Thanks team you made a couple of Kiwis very happy!

Extremely narrow roads which made difficult to get into sites.
Toilet were supposed to be renovated in early 2023 but middle of September not done.

One washing machine for over 500 sites...
Great day for the lil ones, we did the kids digger experience, so much fun and great value for money. So much to do up there.

While the step up itself is great and fun , the education on how to use the machines is lacking and as it states 120 mins with the machines you actually only get 4 mins per area and one area you get 2 mins doing crocs and 4 mins with the tyres . So by the time the kids get to the last one they get 4 mins of actual use because they got 10 mins to figure it out in the first two rounds ( each section . So you get  25 mins at  each section but only get a 4 min turn . So it’s very miss leading when it says 120 mins when in actual fact you only get 4 mins at two sites 6 mins at another and then 4 mins with rc diggers . Half of the staff are friendly and the other few need help with their children skills. All in all it’s a good concept but way to many children in one session for the kids to actually get to enjoy and learn anything . False advertising 100% . It was an experience but not one I’d have my children do again .
Great destination for kids and adults
Good place for kids with adults, thanks.
Amazing place

Awesome park, we did “dig it” however found the groups are so big there is so much down time. Considering this attraction is for considerably younger kids it was hard to occupy them between turns. We had 12 in our group.
Maybe instead of taking in 48 kids over 2 hours you could do groups of 12 over half an hour. Gives plenty of time at each attraction, enough time for induction and less kids in the sandpits. Some kids in our group missed out on a digger then we were delayed because another kid from another group missed out and they allowed them to take a spot in our group.  120 mins of dig it but only 9 minutes of excavation time and 3 mins remote control play 108 mins of playing in sand pit waiting for turns. If it were 30 mins I’d still find the entry fee justified.
Having said that it was a great day and when they were operating they were having an awesome time.

Tree Top 旁邊就是漆彈場
還有迷你高爾夫⛳️
雖然停車空間很有限

Park was great, but I was shocked and extremely disappointed by the by the speed that most employees drove through the park, particularly the long haired gardener in the ute, the white unregistered toyota and the room service staff. One staff member even did a burnout near where the diga staff park and the room service staff applauded!
